America's Coco Gauff claimed just her second ever Major title last year after she beat Belarus' Aryna Sabalenka 6-7, 6-2, 6-4 in the final. programme.title: Live Test Cricket
programme.sub-title: India v Afghanistan-Test Match, Day One
programme.desc: India v Afghanistan. Coverage of the first day of the one-off Test from Maharaja Yadavindra Singh PCA Stadium in Punjab. This is the first meeting between these two sides in this format since 2018 and only the second ever Test match involving the teams. The last meeting saw India make 474 in their only innings, before they bowled Afghanistan out for 109. The visitors then followed on and were bowled out for just 103 as India won by an innings and 262 runs.

programme.title: Live MLB
programme.desc: Detroit Tigers v Seattle Mariners (Start-time 6.10pm). Coverage of the American League match from Comerica Park. These two teams last met in last season's play-offs back in early October as the Mariners won three games to two to progress to the American League Championship series. Detroit won the first game 3-2 before back-to-back wins for Seattle took them to within one game of progressing. The Tigers won the fourth game 9-3 before the Mariners took the series thanks to a 3-2 victory in game five.

programme.title: Serena v Azarenka 2012
programme.desc: After three sets of gruelling competition in the 2012 final of the women's singles, Serena Williams defeated Victoria Azarenka in the first of two back-to-back finals at the US Open.

programme.title: Robson v Na 2012
programme.desc: Having defeated a three-time US Open champion in the second round, Great Britain's Laura Robson came up against China's Li Na in the third round of the women's singles at the 2012 US Open.

programme.title: WTA Final: Rabat
programme.desc: Anhelina Kalinina, competing in her third career final, looks to capture her first title in the Rabat showpiece against first-time finalist, sixth seed Petra Marcinko. (23.

programme.title: ATP Final: Madrid
programme.desc: Eyeing a historic fifth consecutive ATP Masters 1000 title, world number one Jannik Sinner meets familiar rival and two-time Madrid Open champion Alexander Zverev. (03.

programme.title: WTA Final: Auckland
programme.desc: Elina Svitolina competes in her second Auckland final and the 24th of her career overall as the Ukrainian plays seventh seed Xinyu Wang, who features in just her second WTA final. (11.

programme.title: ATP Final: Rome
programme.desc: World number one Jannik Sinner, a win away from completing the Career Golden Masters, meets first-time Rome finalist Casper Ruud - who trails 4-0 in head-to-head meetings. (17.

programme.title: WTA Final: Brisbane
programme.desc: Defending champion Aryna Sabalenka enters a third straight Brisbane final as the world number one meets Marta Kostyuk, who defeated fourth seed Jessica Pegula to get here. (11.

programme.title: ATP Final: Hamburg
programme.desc: Tommy Paul's 10th tour-level final pits the American against Peru's Ignacio Buse, who enters a maiden tour-level title match. Paul was a clay-court champion in Houston in April. (23.

programme.title: ATP/WTA Tour Classic Matches
programme.desc: In a match that would decide the 2016 year-end world number one, Andy Murray - looking to cap a storybook season at The O2 in London - met rival Novak Djokovic at the ATP Finals.

programme.title: Murray's Moments
programme.desc: 961 days and one new hip later, Andy Murray accomplished an incredible feat nine months after career-saving surgery by beating Stan Wawrinka in Antwerp to end a two-year wait for a title.

programme.title: 2023 Djokovic v Alcaraz
programme.desc: Out to avenge the five-set Wimbledon final defeat inflicted by the Spaniard, Novak Djokovic finally overcame Carlos Alcaraz in just under four hours in the 2023 Cincinnati final.

programme.desc: Revisit the 2013 US Open final as Rafael Nadal overpowered Novak Djokovic to win a second Flushing Meadows title.
